extra.reasoning:
focal_weakness appears in exactly one rule: interaction_drop (priority 100, the highest), which LOWERS acuity to self_care. No rule raises acuity from focal_weakness. So flipping focal_weakness false->true can only enable the drop rule.

Counterexample base: rash=T, severe_headache=T, chest_pain=T, vision_loss=F.

With focal_weakness=FALSE: matching rules = raise_chest_pain(urgent,p2), raise_rash(urgent,p2), raise_severe_headache(emergent,p3). interaction_drop does not match (focal_weakness false). Highest priority is raise_severe_headache (p3) => acuity = emergent.

With focal_weakness=TRUE (all else identical): interaction_drop now matches (rash & severe_headache & chest_pain & focal_weakness all true) at priority 100, which dominates raise_severe_headache (p3). => acuity = self_care.

Setting focal_weakness true lowers acuity from emergent to self_care, violating monotonicity. The witness_assignment given is the focal_weakness=true point that exhibits the lowered acuity (self_care), versus its focal_weakness=false sibling (emergent).
